body:
Hello,

Thank you for the quick reply on my group-tour request. To confirm the details: 28 fourth-graders plus 4 chaperones, and we'd prefer a weekday morning in early October. The students have been studying World War II aviation this term, so the warbird gallery is the main draw.

Do you offer a guided docent talk as part of the group rate, or is that booked separately? And is there a covered area where the kids can eat a packed lunch afterward?

Thank you,
Diane Merrill

Hi Diane,

Happy to help. For a group of 32 we can hold a weekday-morning slot in early October — Tuesday the 6th or Thursday the 8th both have a 9:30 entry open. The per-student education rate is $7, and chaperones are admitted free at a ratio of one per eight students, so all four of yours are covered.

A 30-minute docent talk in the warbird gallery is included at no extra charge; just let me know if you'd like it tailored toward the WWII curriculum. We also have a covered courtyard with picnic tables that your group is welcome to use for a packed lunch.

Reply with your preferred date and I'll send the reservation form to lock it in.

Best regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Hello,

Following up on my question about wheelchair access for the DC-3 exhibit. My father uses a wheelchair full-time, and we'd like to bring him this weekend. Could you confirm whether the boarding platform itself is reachable by ramp, or whether the aircraft interior is viewable only from the gallery floor?

We don't want to make the trip and find out he can't get to the part he's most looking forward to.

Thanks,
Greg Santana

Hi Greg,

Thanks for the details — that helps me give you a clear answer. The DC-3 gallery is fully step-free, and there is a switchback ramp up to the boarding platform, so your father can roll right up to the open cabin door and view the interior from the threshold. The cabin aisle itself is too narrow for a wheelchair, but the open door gives a full view of the cockpit and forward seating.

We also keep two loaner wheelchairs at the front desk if you'd like a spare on hand. No reservation needed for the visit, but feel free to ask for me at the desk and I'll make sure a docent is available to walk you through it.

Warm regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Carmen,

That's exactly what I needed to know — thank you. The ramp to the platform is the part that was worrying me, so it's a relief to hear the open cabin door is reachable. We'll plan to come Saturday morning when it's quieter.

I'll ask for you at the front desk. Appreciate you taking the time to spell it all out.

Greg Santana

Hello,

Following up on my membership renewal. My annual membership lapsed last month and I'd like to move up to the family level so my grandkids are covered when they visit. Can I complete the renewal online, or do I need to come to the front desk in person?

If it can be done online, a link would be appreciated.

Best,
Patrick Donnelly

Hi Patrick,

Good news — you can do the whole thing online. I've reactivated your lapsed account and set it ready to upgrade. Just log in at the membership portal, choose Family ($95/year, covers two adults and up to four children or grandchildren), and the system will prorate the difference from your previous level.

If you'd rather not deal with the portal, the front desk can process the same upgrade in about five minutes during gallery hours. Either way your new cards will mail out within a week.

Let me know if the login gives you any trouble.

Best regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Carmen,

Got it done through the portal — the family upgrade went through and I see the prorated charge. One quick question: my grandson is seven and my granddaughter is four. Do the children's cards need their own photos, or are they covered under the family membership without separate cards?

Thanks again for the help.

Patrick Donnelly

Hi Patrick,

No separate cards or photos needed for the children — a family membership covers all named dependents under one account, and the front desk just looks you up by name on arrival. Your two grandchildren are already listed on the renewal you completed.

You're all set. Enjoy your next visit, and the simulator and junior flight activities are both included at the family level.

Warm regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Hello,

Following up on the blue water bottle I think I left near the flight-sim kiosk last Saturday. It's a stainless steel bottle, dark blue, with a dent near the base and a small sticker of a P-51 on the side. We were there in the early afternoon.

Has anything matching that turned up in lost and found? Happy to come by and collect it if so.

Thank you,
Nathan Briggs

Hi Nathan,

Good news — a dark blue stainless bottle with a P-51 sticker was turned in to the front desk Saturday evening and it's sitting in our lost and found now. The dented base matches your description, so I'm confident it's yours.

We'll hold it under your name for 30 days. You can pick it up at the front desk any day during gallery hours, no need to make an appointment. Just mention the lost-and-found ticket and they'll have it ready.

Glad we could reunite you with it.

Best regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Hello,

Following up on my question about hours for the upcoming holiday weekend. I'm planning to bring out-of-town family and want to make sure we time it right. Are the galleries on regular hours that Saturday and Sunday, and is the gift shop open the same hours as the galleries or does it close earlier?

Thanks,
Teresa Vaughn

Hi Teresa,

Happy to confirm. Over the holiday weekend the galleries are open regular hours both days — 9 AM to 5 PM Saturday and Sunday, with last entry at 4 PM. We are closed on the Monday holiday itself.

The gift shop opens with the galleries at 9 AM but closes 30 minutes earlier, at 4:30 PM, to allow time to cash out. If you're hoping to browse the shop, I'd plan to swing through before the galleries close rather than after.

Let me know if you'd like anything else for the visit.

Best regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Carmen,

That's perfect, thank you. We'll come Saturday morning and hit the gift shop first so we don't miss it. One more thing — is there a cafe or anywhere to grab coffee on site, or should we plan to bring our own?

Appreciate the clear answer.

Teresa Vaughn

Hello,

Following up on the private-event rental I asked about for the WWII gallery. We're looking at an evening reception for roughly 40 guests, mostly seated, with a short speaking program. Could you send pricing and a list of available evening dates this fall?

We'd also need to know whether outside catering is allowed or whether you have a preferred vendor.

Regards,
Diane Merrill

Hi Diane,

Thanks for the details — a 40-guest evening reception is well within the WWII gallery's capacity. After-hours gallery rental is $1,200 for a four-hour block (6 to 10 PM), which includes setup access, basic AV for your speaking program, and one staff host. A short list of open Friday and Saturday evenings this October and November is attached.

Outside catering is welcome; we just ask that you use a licensed caterer and provide a certificate of insurance two weeks out. We also keep a list of three local caterers who know the space, if that's easier.

Let me know a date or two that interest you and I'll place a courtesy hold.

Best regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Carmen,

This is very helpful. The Friday evening dates in October look best for us — let's hold the first available Friday if we can. We'll plan to bring in our own caterer, so please send that certificate-of-insurance requirement in writing so I can pass it along to them.

One last question: is the cockpit display roped off during private events, or can guests get close to it? Several of our attendees are former service members and would appreciate seeing it up close.

Regards,
Diane Merrill

Hi Diane,

Done — I've placed a courtesy hold on the first open Friday in October and emailed you the rental agreement along with the catering insurance requirement in writing. The hold lasts ten days, which gives your caterer time to get the certificate to us.

During private events the cockpit display stays open and accessible; we only ask that guests not climb onto the boarding step. Our staff host can give a short walk-up talk on the aircraft if your veterans would enjoy that — just let me know and I'll arrange a docent for the evening.

Warm regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Hello,

Following up on the summer aviation camp for my grandson. He's nine and absolutely set on it after his last visit. Could you confirm the age range you accept and whether there's still space in the session you mentioned? He'd be coming on his own, so I'd also like to know the daily drop-off and pickup times.

Thank you,
Teresa Vaughn

Hi Teresa,

Great news — your grandson is right in the range. The aviation camp accepts ages 8 to 12, and there are still four spots open in the upcoming session. Drop-off is 8:30 AM and pickup is 3:30 PM, Monday through Friday, with an optional supervised hour until 4:30 PM if you need it.

The week covers flight-simulator time, a build-your-own glider workshop, and a behind-the-ropes tour of the restoration shop. I've attached the registration form and the supply list. Spots tend to fill quickly once we confirm, so I'd suggest getting the form back this week to hold his place.

Best regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Hello,

Following up on becoming a volunteer docent. I'm recently retired and spend a lot of time at the museum already, so I'd like to give back by leading tours. Could you tell me what the training schedule looks like and how I go about applying?

I'm most interested in the warbird and WWII galleries if there's any say in placement.

Thank you,
Marcus Webb

Hi Marcus,

Wonderful — we're always glad to bring on docents who already love the place. The application is a short online form (link attached) plus a brief interview with our volunteer coordinator. Once you're accepted, training runs over four Saturday mornings and covers gallery content, tour delivery, and a shadowing shift with an experienced docent before you lead on your own.

We do take gallery preference into account, and the warbird and WWII galleries are both areas where we can use more help, so your interest there is a good fit. The next training cohort starts in a few weeks.

Reply once you've submitted the form and I'll have the coordinator reach out to schedule your interview.

Best regards,
Carmen Flores
Visitor Services, Aviation Heritage Museum

Carmen,

Form submitted. Thank you for laying it all out so clearly — four Saturdays and a shadow shift sounds very manageable, and I'm glad to hear the warbird gallery needs the help. I drove my 4Runner over and picked up the supply list at the desk while I was there.

I'll watch for the coordinator's email about the interview. Looking forward to getting started.

Marcus Webb
